walkthrough.title: Unfold the Village Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Horrified set up. First, unfold the village board and place it in the center of the table. This board shows an interconnected map of locations including the Church, Graveyard, Manor, Laboratory, Docks, and Camp. These locations are connected by paths that heroes and monsters will travel along.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Monsters
walkthrough.text: Now let's pick the monsters you will face. For your first game, we recommend starting with just one monster. Two monsters is the standard difficulty, and three monsters is a real challenge. The six available monsters are Dracula, Frankenstein's Monster, the Bride of Frankenstein, the Wolf Man, the Invisible Man, and the Creature from the Black Lagoon.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Monster Mats and Tokens
walkthrough.text: For each monster you chose, grab their monster mat and figure. The mat shows the monster's unique defeat conditions. Place each monster figure on its starting location as shown on the mat. Then set up any special tokens. Dracula needs coffin tokens on marked locations. Frankenstein and the Bride need body part tokens scattered around. The Creature needs tokens at water locations.

walkthrough.title: Set the Terror Track
walkthrough.text: Find the terror track on the board and set the marker to zero. The terror track goes up to seven, and if it ever reaches seven, your team loses immediately. Terror increases when villagers are attacked or certain monster events trigger. Keep a close eye on this track throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the item cards and place them in a face-down deck. Then shuffle the monster cards and place them in a separate face-down deck. The size of the monster deck varies based on your player count. Place the villager tokens in a supply near the board.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Heroes
walkthrough.text: Each player picks one hero from the seven available. Each hero has a unique special ability. The Mayor can give items to distant heroes. The Inspector gets extra actions near monsters. The Courier has extra movement. Read through the hero cards and pick one that sounds fun to you. Place your hero figure on your hero's starting location.

walkthrough.title: Draw Starting Items
walkthrough.text: Each player draws starting item cards based on the player count. Fewer players get more starting items to compensate. Items are color-coded and will be used to complete monster defeat tasks. Keep your items visible so your team can coordinate.

walkthrough.title: Ready to Play
walkthrough.text: Pick a random first player. Remember, this is a cooperative game. Everyone works together against the monsters. Your win condition is to defeat all selected monsters. Your lose condition is the terror track reaching seven. Talk openly about your plans and coordinate your actions. Let's learn the rules.

walkthrough.title: Game Overview
walkthrough.text: Horrified is a cooperative game where your team of heroes works together to defeat classic Universal monsters before the village is overrun with terror. Each turn you take actions with your hero, then a monster card triggers events and monster movement. Your goal is to defeat all selected monsters before the terror track reaches seven.

walkthrough.title: Your Turn: Two Phases
walkthrough.text: Each player's turn has two phases. First is the Hero Phase, where you spend your actions to move, collect items, and work toward defeating monsters. Second is the Monster Phase, where you draw a monster card that places new items, triggers events, and moves the monsters. Let's look at each phase in detail.

walkthrough.title: Hero Phase: Your Actions
walkthrough.text: During the Hero Phase, you get a number of actions, typically three or four depending on player count. You can spend these actions in any combination. Move one space along a path for one action. Pick up an item at your location for one action. Share an item with another hero at your location for one action. Guide a villager one space toward safety for one action.

walkthrough.title: Advancing Monster Tasks
walkthrough.text: The most important action is advancing a monster's defeat task. Each monster has a unique set of conditions that must be met to defeat it. You spend items at specific locations to make progress. For example, Dracula requires you to deliver items to his coffin locations. Spending one item toward a task costs one action.

walkthrough.title: Hero Special Abilities
walkthrough.text: Do not forget your hero's special ability. Each hero has a unique power that bends the normal rules. The Mayor can pass items to heroes at distant locations. The Courier gets extra movement. The Scientist uses items more efficiently. Using your ability well is the difference between a smooth victory and a narrow defeat.

walkthrough.title: Monster Phase: Drawing a Card
walkthrough.text: After your hero actions, draw a monster card and resolve it in order. First, place new item tokens at the locations shown on the card. Second, resolve any special event printed on the card. Third, move each active monster toward the nearest hero or villager. Different monsters move at different speeds.

walkthrough.title: Monster Attacks
walkthrough.text: If a monster ends up in the same location as a hero, it attacks. The hero must discard items to absorb the attack. If a hero has no items to discard, they are defeated and respawn, but the terror track advances. If a monster reaches a villager, the villager is removed and the terror track advances.

walkthrough.title: Defeating Monsters
walkthrough.text: Each monster has unique defeat conditions. Dracula requires destroying his coffins with items, then confronting him. Frankenstein and the Bride need body parts delivered to the Laboratory. The Creature requires colored items at water locations in sequence. The Wolf Man is confronted directly with items. The Invisible Man requires setting traps at locations.

walkthrough.title: The Terror Track
walkthrough.text: The terror track is your team's clock. It advances when villagers are attacked, when heroes are defeated, and when certain monster card events trigger. If it reaches seven, you lose immediately. Managing terror is just as important as working on monster defeat conditions. Sometimes you need to drop what you are doing and rescue a villager.

walkthrough.title: Item Management
walkthrough.text: Items come in different colors, and monster defeat conditions often require specific colors at specific locations. You can hold four to five items at a time. Sharing items between heroes costs an action but is essential for getting the right items to the right places. Plan your item logistics as a team.

walkthrough.title: Guiding Villagers
walkthrough.text: Villagers appear on the board when monster cards place them. They cannot move on their own. If a monster reaches a villager's location, the villager is removed and terror advances. You must spend actions to guide villagers to safe locations. Each action moves a villager one space along a path.

walkthrough.title: Winning and Losing
walkthrough.text: Your team wins by defeating all selected monsters before the terror track reaches seven. The game is cooperative, so everyone wins or loses together. There is no point scoring. Difficulty scales based on the number and combination of monsters you choose. Start with one monster and work your way up as your team improves.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Village
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first game of Horrified! In this practice tutorial, we will walk through several turns of a cooperative game. We will use two heroes facing one monster, Dracula, to keep things manageable. Follow along and you will see how the hero and monster phases work together.

walkthrough.title: Meet the Heroes
walkthrough.text: Let's say Player 1 is the Courier, who gets extra movement, and Player 2 is the Scientist, who uses items more efficiently. Both heroes start at their designated locations on the board. Dracula is lurking at his starting location with coffin tokens placed around the village.

walkthrough.title: Understanding Dracula's Defeat Condition
walkthrough.text: Before we start, let's check Dracula's monster mat. To defeat Dracula, you need to deliver items to each of his four coffin locations to destroy them, then confront Dracula directly with remaining items. This means you need to plan routes that pass through coffin locations while carrying the right colored items.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's First Turn: Collecting Items
walkthrough.text: Player 1, the Courier, has three actions. Let's use the first action to move one space toward the nearest item location. Use the second action to pick up an item token at that location. Use the third action to move one more space toward the first coffin location. The Courier's extra movement ability lets you cover ground quickly.

walkthrough.title: The Monster Phase
walkthrough.text: After Player 1 finishes their hero actions, draw a monster card. The card shows where to place new item tokens on the board. Then resolve any events. Finally, move Dracula toward the nearest hero or villager. Dracula moves one or two spaces depending on the card. Keep track of where he ends up.

walkthrough.title: Player 2's Turn: Advancing the Task
walkthrough.text: Player 2, the Scientist, moves toward a coffin location and spends items there to destroy the coffin. The Scientist's ability makes item use more efficient, so they can accomplish more with fewer items. After spending the items, that coffin is destroyed. One down, three to go.

walkthrough.title: A Villager Appears
walkthrough.text: During the monster phase after Player 2's turn, the monster card places a villager token on the board. This villager is now in danger because Dracula will move toward the nearest hero or villager. If Dracula reaches the villager, terror advances. Someone needs to guide this villager to safety.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Ignoring the Villager
walkthrough.text: Player 1 decides to keep pushing toward the next coffin instead of guiding the villager. Dracula moves toward the unprotected villager. On the next monster phase, Dracula reaches the villager and the terror track goes up by one. That is one step closer to losing the game.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Protect the Villagers
walkthrough.text: Never ignore villagers. The terror track is your clock, and every villager attacked brings you one step closer to seven and losing the game. Even if it costs you a turn of progress on the monster task, guiding a villager to safety is almost always worth it. One point of terror now can snowball into a loss later.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Hoarding Items
walkthrough.text: Player 2 has been collecting items but not spending them. They have four items in hand and are moving toward a coffin, but they pass through another coffin location on the way. They could spend items here now, but they want to save them for the final confrontation with Dracula. Meanwhile, time is running out.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Spend Items When You Can
walkthrough.text: Do not hoard items for the perfect moment. If you are at a coffin location with the right items, spend them now. Every turn you hold items instead of using them is a turn the terror track could advance. Efficiency means delivering items the moment you have the opportunity, not stockpiling for later.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Poor Coordination
walkthrough.text: Both players decide to head toward the same coffin location. They arrive at the same spot with overlapping items while two other coffins sit untouched across the board. They have doubled up their efforts when they should have split up to cover more ground.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Divide and Conquer
walkthrough.text: Talk to your teammates and divide responsibilities. In a cooperative game, the biggest mistake is working on the same task when you could split up and cover twice as much ground. Assign each hero a region of the board or a specific monster task to maximize your team's efficiency.

walkthrough.title: Destroying the Final Coffin
walkthrough.text: After coordinating properly, the heroes split up and systematically destroy the remaining coffins. Player 1 uses the Courier's speed to deliver items to distant coffins while Player 2 handles the nearby ones. All four coffins are now destroyed, and it is time to confront Dracula directly.

walkthrough.title: Confronting Dracula
walkthrough.text: With all coffins destroyed, a hero needs to be at Dracula's location with enough items to defeat him. Player 2 moves to Dracula's location and spends the required items. Dracula is defeated! One monster down. If you had chosen multiple monsters, you would continue until all are defeated.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Congratulations! You have walked through your first practice game of Horrified. You have seen how to collect items, advance monster tasks, handle villagers, and learned from three common mistakes: ignoring villagers, hoarding items, and poor coordination. You are ready to face more monsters.

walkthrough.title: Before You Face the Darkness
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your next game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics on managing terror and coordinating your team.

Second, reference the Appendix for details on each monster's defeat conditions.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we dive into strategy, here are the most common mistakes in Horrified. First, ignoring the terror track and focusing only on monster tasks while villagers are attacked. Second, hoarding items in your hand instead of spending them at task locations. Third, poor team coordination where everyone works on the same task instead of splitting up. Fourth, forgetting to use your hero's special ability. Keep these in mind to avoid the biggest cooperative game traps.

walkthrough.title: Terror Management is Priority One
walkthrough.text: The terror track is your team's countdown clock. Every point of terror brings you closer to an instant loss at seven. Protecting villagers is not a distraction from defeating monsters. It is an essential part of your strategy. When a villager appears on the board, immediately assess who is closest and assign them to guide the villager to safety. A well-managed terror track gives your team the breathing room to defeat monsters methodically.

walkthrough.title: Plan Item Routes
walkthrough.text: The core puzzle in Horrified is item logistics. Items spawn at specific locations, monster tasks require specific colors at specific places, and heroes have limited carrying capacity. Before you start moving, plan a route that picks up items and delivers them to task locations in the fewest possible turns. The best players chain pickups and deliveries in a single trip rather than making separate collection and delivery runs.

walkthrough.title: Use Hero Abilities Aggressively
walkthrough.text: Each hero ability is designed to give your team a significant advantage, but only if you use it. The Mayor's remote item sharing can save multiple turns of travel. The Courier's extra movement lets them cover the entire board. The Scientist's efficient item use stretches your limited resources. Identify what makes each hero special and build your team's plan around maximizing those abilities every single turn.

walkthrough.title: Defeat the Most Dangerous Monster First
walkthrough.text: When facing multiple monsters, do not spread your efforts evenly. Identify which monster is the most dangerous. Usually this is the one that moves fastest, attacks most frequently, or has the hardest-to-manage conditions. Focus your team on defeating that monster first to reduce the pressure, then move on to the remaining threats. Eliminating one monster simplifies the entire game.

walkthrough.title: Coordinate Through Communication
walkthrough.text: Horrified is a cooperative game, so talk to your teammates. Share what items you have, where you are heading, and what you plan to do on your next turn. Before anyone takes their turn, the whole team should agree on priorities. Is terror rising too fast? Someone should handle villagers. Is a coffin about to be in range? Someone should prepare items for it. Open communication is your best weapon.

walkthrough.title: Manage Your Hand Size
walkthrough.text: You can only carry four to five items at a time. If your hand is full, you cannot pick up new items, which means items sitting on the board are wasted. Conversely, if your hand is empty, you are vulnerable to monster attacks because you have nothing to discard. Aim to keep two or three items in hand at all times. Spend items at task locations regularly to make room for new pickups.

walkthrough.title: Difficulty Scaling Tips
walkthrough.text: For your first game, start with one monster to learn the mechanics. Once you win consistently, add a second monster. Three monsters is where the real challenge begins and requires near-perfect coordination. When choosing monster combinations, know that some pairings are harder than others. Dracula plus the Wolf Man is a good starting combination for two monsters. The Invisible Man adds a unique puzzle element that changes how you think about positioning.

bullets: Don't ignore terror track — villager attacks add up fast
Don't hoard items — spend them at task locations
Don't cluster — split responsibilities across the board
Don't forget hero abilities — they're your biggest advantage

summary.title: Difficulty Scaling Tips
bullets: Start with 1 monster to learn mechanics
2 monsters = standard challenge; Dracula + Wolf Man is good combo
3 monsters = near-perfect coordination required
Some monster pairings are harder than others

walkthrough.title: Monster Priority Analysis
walkthrough.text: When facing multiple monsters, analyze which one threatens the most terror. Monsters that move faster or interact with villagers more frequently should be your first target. Dracula's coffin mechanic spreads across the board, requiring lots of travel. The Wolf Man's erratic movement makes him unpredictable. The Invisible Man requires a completely different approach with trap placement. Assess each monster's threat level and build your plan accordingly.

walkthrough.title: Hero Synergies
walkthrough.text: Certain hero combinations are stronger than others. The Courier and Mayor together create an item delivery network where the Courier collects items quickly and the Mayor redistributes them to where they are needed. The Inspector pairs well with any hero heading toward a monster because the extra actions near monsters accelerate defeat conditions. Choose heroes whose abilities complement each other and the specific monsters you are facing.

walkthrough.title: Optimal Item Routing
walkthrough.text: The difference between winning and losing often comes down to movement efficiency. Map out the shortest paths between item spawn points and task locations. When multiple routes are available, choose the one that passes through the most useful locations. Consider whether a slightly longer route that picks up an extra item or guides a villager along the way is actually more efficient overall.

walkthrough.title: Monster Movement Manipulation
walkthrough.text: Monsters move toward the nearest hero or villager. You can use this to your advantage by positioning your heroes strategically. If you want to keep a monster away from a villager, position a hero closer to the monster so it moves toward you instead. You can also bait monsters toward locations where you have already set up defenses or where their defeat conditions can be advanced.

walkthrough.title: Terror Budget Planning
walkthrough.text: With a maximum of seven terror before you lose, treat terror as a resource to budget. In a game with two monsters, you can afford perhaps three or four points of terror across the entire game. That means you might intentionally let one villager be attacked early if it allows you to make critical progress on a monster defeat condition. This is an advanced judgment call, but sometimes controlled terror is better than lost tempo.

walkthrough.title: Three-Monster Strategies
walkthrough.text: Three-monster games require a fundamentally different approach. You cannot afford to tackle monsters one at a time because the combined terror pressure will overwhelm you. Instead, advance multiple monsters' defeat conditions simultaneously. Assign each hero a primary monster with a secondary responsibility. Use the first few turns to establish positions near task locations and then execute in parallel.

walkthrough.title: Perk Card Timing
walkthrough.text: Perk cards provide powerful one-time effects that can turn the tide of the game. The key is timing. Do not use a perk card the moment you get it. Save it for a critical moment when it provides maximum value, such as when you need to rescue a villager who is about to be attacked, or when you need one extra action to complete a monster's defeat condition.

walkthrough.title: Monster-Specific Deep Dives
walkthrough.text: Each monster rewards a different strategic approach. Dracula demands board-wide logistics and route planning. Frankenstein and the Bride reward methodical token collection with a central delivery point. The Creature requires strict color management at water locations. The Wolf Man needs flexible positioning due to his erratic movement. The Invisible Man turns the game into a trap-laying puzzle. Study each monster's mechanics and draft your team plan before the first turn.

Dracula: spread out; Wolf Man: erratic; Invisible Man: traps
Build plan around threat assessment

summary.title: Monster-Specific Deep Dives
bullets: Dracula: board-wide logistics and route planning
Frankenstein/Bride: methodical collection, central delivery
Creature: strict color management at water locations
Wolf Man: flexible positioning; Invisible Man: trap puzzle

entries.definition: A monster who requires destroying four coffin tokens placed at locations across the board. Deliver colored items to each coffin location to destroy it, then confront Dracula directly with items to defeat him.
A monster who requires collecting scattered body part tokens and delivering them to the Laboratory. Paired with the Bride of Frankenstein. Reuniting the monsters at the laboratory defeats both.
A monster paired with Frankenstein's Monster. Both are defeated together by collecting body part tokens and delivering them to the Laboratory. They share a combined defeat condition.
A monster who moves erratically based on frenzy tokens. Defeated by collecting items and confronting him directly. His unpredictable movement makes him hard to corner.
A monster defeated by setting traps at locations using items. When the Invisible Man moves onto a trapped location, he becomes visible and can be defeated. Requires strategic trap placement.
A monster defeated by delivering specific colored items to marked water locations in a particular sequence. Requires careful color management and route planning.
Hero who can give items to heroes at distant locations without being in the same space. Excellent for item logistics and redistribution across the board.
Hero who gains extra actions when near monsters. Best positioned close to active monster threats to maximize action efficiency.
Hero who moves items more efficiently. Specializes in item logistics and delivery optimization.
Hero with extra movement capability. Can cover the entire board quickly, making them ideal for urgent villager rescues and long-distance item delivery.
Hero who can move through blocked paths. Bypasses movement restrictions that other heroes face, providing alternative routes across the board.
Hero who uses items more efficiently. Gets more value per item spent when advancing monster defeat conditions.
Hero with combat bonuses. More effective when confronting monsters directly and can handle monster attacks more easily.
A track on the board going from zero to seven. Advances when villagers are attacked, heroes are defeated, or certain card events trigger. Reaching seven means the team immediately loses.
A card drawn during the Monster Phase of each turn. Shows where to place new item tokens, any special events to trigger, and how monsters move this turn.
Color-coded items used to advance monster defeat conditions and absorb monster attacks. Heroes can hold four to five items at a time. Sharing items between heroes costs one action.
A one-time use card that provides a powerful special effect. Gained through certain conditions or monster-specific events. Should be saved for critical moments.

A civilian token placed on the board by monster cards. Cannot move on its own. Must be guided to safety by heroes spending actions. If a monster reaches a villager, it is removed and the terror track advances.
The first phase of a player's turn where they spend actions to move, collect items, share items, guide villagers, and advance monster tasks.
The second phase of a player's turn. Draw a monster card, place items, resolve events, and move monsters toward the nearest hero or villager.
A reference sheet for each monster showing its starting location, unique defeat conditions, and any special rules for that monster's behavior.
A Dracula-specific token placed on marked locations. Must be destroyed by delivering colored items to the coffin location. Part of Dracula's defeat condition.
A Frankenstein-specific token scattered across the board. Must be collected and delivered to the Laboratory. Part of the combined Frankenstein and Bride defeat condition.
A Wolf Man-specific token that affects his movement pattern. Makes the Wolf Man's movement erratic and unpredictable.
